api/spec/packages/aip/src/customers/char.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for customer charge API (flat-fee and usage-based charge types). Defines the discriminated union `Charge`, its models, enums, list-operation with filter/sort/expand, and compiles into the v3 OpenAPI spec and generated SDKs.

patterns.name: Generic ChargeBase spread
patterns.description: Charge type models spread `ChargeBase<T>` (itself spreading `Shared.Resource`) to inherit all shared fields. Type-specific fields are added directly on the concrete model.

patterns.name: Discriminated union via @discriminated
patterns.description: `Charge` is a `union` with `@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })`. Variants are named by their discriminator value (`flat_fee`, `usage_based`).
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Charge { flat_fee: FlatFeeCharge, usage_based: UsageBasedCharge }

patterns.name: @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) for all response-only fields
patterns.description: Fields only returned in responses (status, invoice_at, customer, etc.) carry `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)`. Fields writeable on create carry `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create)`.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every model and enum
patterns.description: All models and enums carry `@friendlyName("Billing<Name>")` so generator-level names are stable and prefixed with `Billing`.

patterns.name: Operations file owns @get interface, imports HTTP decorators
patterns.description: `operations.tsp` imports `@typespec/http`, `@typespec/openapi`, declares `using TypeSpec.Http; using TypeSpec.OpenAPI;`, defines filter models and the operations interface. Types stay in `charges.tsp`.

patterns.name: deepObject explode for filter query params
patterns.description: Filter query params use `@query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true })` to support bracket-style filters like `filter[status][oeq]=created`.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all charge models and enums (`ChargeType`, `ChargeStatus`, `ChargeBase<T>`, `FlatFeeCharge`, `UsageBasedCharge`, `Charge` union, `ChargesExpand`).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add HTTP decorators here; those belong in operations.tsp. Concrete models must spread `ChargeBase<T>` — do not duplicate shared fields.

key_file_guides.role: Declares `CustomerChargesOperations` interface with `list` operation, filter/sort/expand query params, and pagination response type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import `@typespec/http` and declare `using TypeSpec.Http;` — otherwise `@get`, `@path`, `@query` decorators are unknown. Response type must use `Shared.PagePaginatedResponse<Charge>` not a raw array.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports charges.tsp and operations.tsp in order; consumed by the parent package index.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files added to this folder must be imported here or they are invisible to the compiler.

anti_patterns: Adding HTTP decorators (@get, @post, @path, @query) in charges.tsp — they belong only in operations.tsp
Defining type-specific fields directly on ChargeBase instead of on FlatFeeCharge / UsageBasedCharge
Omitting @friendlyName on new models or enums — the generated SDK name will be unstable
Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml — always regenerate via `make gen-api`
Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Update) — charges have no update operation; use Read or Create only

decisions.decision: Discriminated union with envelope:none and discriminatorPropertyName:type
decisions.rationale: Keeps the wire format flat (no extra wrapper object) while still allowing the OpenAPI discriminator to route deserialization to the correct model.
Avoids duplicating the large set of shared read-only fields across FlatFeeCharge and UsageBasedCharge while keeping the type parameter visible in the generated schema.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new charge type (e.g. credit_purchase)

api/spec/packages/aip/src/customers/cred.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the customer credits API: credit grants (allocation, funding methods, lifecycle), credit balances, credit transactions (ledger), and credit adjustments. Compiles into v3 OpenAPI spec; gated behind credits.enabled in Go wiring.

patterns.name: Shared.Resource spread for mutable resources, Shared.ResourceImmutable for transactions
patterns.description: `CreditGrant` spreads `Shared.Resource` (mutable); `CreditTransaction` spreads `Shared.ResourceImmutable` to signal no updates are allowed.

patterns.name: Nested anonymous object literals for compound fields
patterns.description: Tightly related sub-fields use inline anonymous objects (`purchase?: { currency: ...; amount: ...; }`) instead of named models, unless the sub-type needs to be referenced elsewhere.
patterns.example: purchase?: { currency: Shared.CurrencyCode; per_unit_cost_basis?: Shared.Numeric; amount: Shared.Numeric; };

patterns.name: @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") on every exported model/enum
patterns.description: All exported symbols carry `@friendlyName("Billing<PascalCase>")` for stable generated SDK names.

patterns.name: Separate interfaces per resource in operations.tsp
patterns.description: Operations are split into fine-grained interfaces: `CustomerCreditGrantsOperations`, `CustomerCreditBalancesOperations`, `CustomerCreditTransactionOperations`, `CustomerCreditAdjustmentsOperations`. No omnibus interface.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination for transactions, page pagination for grants
@visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create) — no Update visibility
patterns.description: No field uses Lifecycle.Update because credits do not have a general PATCH operation; grants have only a targeted `updateExternalSettlement` action.

key_file_guides.watch_for: Several commented-out fields exist (effective_at, expires_after, expires_at, OnAuthorization/OnSettlement enum variants) — these are intentional stubs for future features; do not uncomment without a corresponding Go implementation.

key_file_guides.role: Declares all CRUD and action interfaces for grants, balance, transactions, and adjustments. Imports all sub-type files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import `@typespec/http` and declare `using TypeSpec.Http; using TypeSpec.OpenAPI;`. The void-grant interface is entirely commented out — do not expose it until the Go service implements the void lifecycle.

key_file_guides.role: Defines `CreditBalances` (list of per-currency `CreditBalance`) returned by the balance GET endpoint.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Does not spread Shared.Resource — it is a computed read-only aggregate, not a stored entity. Do not add id/created_at.

key_file_guides.role: Defines `CreditTransaction` (immutable ledger entry) and `CreditTransactionType` enum. Also has commented-out actor model for future attribution.
key_file_guides.watch_for: `CreditTransaction` spreads `Shared.ResourceImmutable` — do not add any mutable or Create-visible fields here.

key_file_guides.role: Defines `CreditAdjustment` for manual balance corrections. Spreads a subset of Shared.Resource fields via `PickProperties`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only Create-visible fields are present; no Read-only state fields. Currency is @visibility(Lifecycle.Create) only.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports grant, ledger, and operations in order. Note: balance.tsp and adjustment.tsp are NOT re-exported from index.tsp — they are imported directly in operations.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New type files must be imported either in index.tsp or directly in operations.tsp before they become available to the compiler.

anti_patterns: Uncommenting void-grant or external-payment-initial-settlement-status stubs without a matching Go charges.Service implementation
Adding @visibility(Lifecycle.Update) — no general update operation exists for credit resources
Spreading Shared.Resource into balance.tsp models — balance is a computed aggregate, not a stored entity
Mixing pagination types: transactions must use cursor pagination, grants/adjustments use page pagination
Hand-editing api/v3/api.gen.go or api/v3/openapi.yaml instead of running `make gen-api`

decisions.decision: Cursor pagination for credit transactions, page pagination for grants
decisions.rationale: Transactions are append-only and high-volume; cursor pagination is stable under concurrent inserts. Grants are low-cardinality and need offset-based navigation.

decisions.decision: Separate operations interfaces per resource sub-type
decisions.rationale: Matches the Go side where each resource maps to a distinct service method group, and makes it easy to enable/disable individual endpoint groups (e.g. void-grant is fully commented out until implemented).

decisions.decision: credits API gated at wiring layer, not at TypeSpec level
decisions.rationale: The TypeSpec spec is always compiled; the credits.enabled flag disables Go handler registration in api/v3/server and noop-wires ledger services in app/common. This keeps the spec complete while the feature is in progress.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new filtered list operation for credit grants by feature
